headline: MU, SNDK, DRAM Dip Overnight As Memory Stocks Feel Weight Of Samsung’s Post-Earnings Selloff
summary: Samsung’s stock slid in Korea even as the company posted stellar Q2 preliminary results; retail traders believe strong performance was already priced into the shares.

headline: Does Western Digital’s (WDC) Shift Into Growth Indexes Recast Its Core AI Storage Narrative?
summary: On 27 June 2026, Western Digital Corporation was reclassified across multiple Russell indexes, moving from several value benchmarks into major growth and top‑tier large-cap indexes including the Russell 1000 Growth, Russell Top 200, and Russell 3000 Growth series. This shift toward growth-oriented benchmarks reflects how index providers now categorize Western Digital’s business profile, with AI-linked storage demand and hyperscaler exposure increasingly defining its role in the broader...

content.title: Does Western Digital’s (WDC) Shift Into Growth Indexes Recast Its Core AI Storage Narrative?

headline: US futures edge lower as investors assess Samsung results and Fed outlook: Dow Jones, S&P, Nasdaq, Wall Street
summary: US stock index futures traded cautiously on Tuesday after the Dow Jones Industrial Average reached a record high in the previous session, while investors weighed another strong earnings update from Samsung Electronics (USOTC:SSNHZ) alongside fresh comments from Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller. Futures pause after Wall Street recordsBy 03:02 ET (07:02 GMT), futures linked to the Dow Jones Industrial Average were little changed.

headline: Sandisk Stock Was Red Hot but Get Set for a Cold Snap
summary: Sandisk  has been one of the hottest U.S. tech stocks over the past year but it’s starting to cool off.  Shares of the memory storage company were down around 6% ahead of the open Tuesday, among the sharpest fallers in the  The stock, up 635% this year, has fallen 23% over the past three trading days.

headline: SK Hynix’s $29 Billion Listing Puts the Memory Supercycle to the Test

headline: Micron, SanDisk, and Western Digital Sink 7% as Samsung Earnings Spark a Memory Selloff
summary: Memory and storage stocks are selling off sharply in early trading Tuesday, reversing Monday’s rebound. Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) shares are down 7% to $917, SanDisk (NASDAQ:SNDK) stock is off 7% to $1,616, and Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) shares are dropping 7% to $537. The selling extends across the group. Seagate Technology (NASDAQ:STX) stock is down 5% ... Micron, SanDisk, and Western Digital Sink 7% as Samsung Earnings Spark a Memory Selloff
